 {"id":"bd-1iy","title":"Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Gemini provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/gemini.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Gemini provider (Google Generative AI):
- generateContent endpoint with streaming
- Function declarations (tools)
- 631 lines, 4 unit tests

## Gemini-Specific Considerations
1. **Streaming URL**: Uses ?alt=sse query parameter
2. **Response Format**: Different JSON structure from Anthropic/OpenAI
3. **Tool Format**: functionDeclarations in tools array
4. **Content Parts**: text, functionCall, functionResponse

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa, bd-pwz, bd-37l (follow Anthropic pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports
- [ ] 4 tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:08.130271194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.665851795Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-37l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.665829753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:08.138703827Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1iy","depends_on_id":"bd-pwz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:41.413699713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.861448590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101819926Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:46.870643664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mh","depends_on_id":"bd-3nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:59.101795250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qk","title":"Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity","description":"# Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature/functionality coverage of the original Pi Agent (TypeScript) with:
- Complete conformance harness validating behavioral equivalence
- Comprehensive test coverage without mocks where possible
- Full integration with sibling libraries (asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust)
- Detailed E2E integration test scripts with verbose logging

## Background
pi_agent_rust is a Rust port of Pi Agent (TypeScript CLI) by Mario Zechner. The port uses
spec-first methodology: extract behavior from legacy → implement from spec → never translate
line-by-line. Currently at ~85% feature complete with all core functionality working.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-02)
- ✅ Core Types: All message types, content blocks, usage tracking
- ✅ 7 Built-in Tools: read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls (122 conformance tests)
- ✅ Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure (all streaming + tool use)
- ✅ Agent Runtime: Full loop with tool iteration and event callbacks
- ✅ Session Management: JSONL v3 format, tree structure, branching
- ✅ Interactive TUI: Elm Architecture with bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour
- ✅ CLI: Full argument parsing, subcommands, package management
- ✅ Authentication: auth.json storage, OAuth login/logout
- ✅ Compaction: Full context compaction with LLM summarization
- ✅ RPC Mode: Complete JSON protocol implementation
- ✅ Benchmarks: Truncation, SSE parsing with performance budgets

## Key Gaps Requiring Work
1. **HTTP Migration**: Providers still use reqwest; asupersync HTTP client stubbed
2. **Extensions Runtime**: Protocol defined but no WASM loader/runtime
3. **Session Index Integration**: Implemented but not wired into CLI
4. **Themes**: Not implemented
5. **Test Coverage**: Critical gaps in provider streaming, TUI, CLI, compaction

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All providers use asupersync HTTP client (tokio removed)
- [ ] Extension runtime loads and executes WASM extensions
- [ ] Session picker uses SQLite index for fast lookups
- [ ] Theme system loads and applies themes
- [ ] 95%+ test coverage without mocks for core paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real API calls (recorded/VCR style)
- [ ] All conformance fixtures pass
- [ ] Performance targets met (startup <100ms, binary <20MB)

## Workstreams (child features)
1. HTTP Migration (HIGH priority) - Blocks tokio removal
2. Test Coverage Expansion (HIGH priority) - Quality gate
3. Session Index Integration (MEDIUM priority) - UX improvement
4. Themes (LOW priority) - Nice to have
5. Extensions Runtime (tracked separately in existing beads bd-25q)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-37a","title":"Implement theme file format and loader","description":"# Implement theme file format and loader

## Goal
Define theme JSON schema and implement theme file loading.

## Implementation

### Theme Types (src/theme.rs)
```rust
use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct Theme {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub colors: ThemeColors,
    pub syntax: SyntaxColors,
    pub ui: UiColors,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct ThemeColors {
    pub foreground: String,
    pub background: String,
    pub accent: String,
    pub success: String,
    pub warning: String,
    pub error: String,
    pub muted: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct SyntaxColors {
    pub keyword: String,
    pub string: String,
    pub number: String,
    pub comment: String,
    pub function: String,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Serialize)]
pub struct UiColors {
    pub border: String,
    pub selection: String,
    pub cursor: String,
}
```

### Theme Discovery
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn discover_themes() -> Vec<PathBuf> {
        let mut paths = Vec::new();
        
        // Global themes
        if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {
            let global = home.join(".pi/agent/themes");
            if global.exists() {
                paths.extend(glob_json(&global));
            }
        }
        
        // Project themes
        let project = PathBuf::from(".pi/themes");
        if project.exists() {
            paths.extend(glob_json(&project));
        }
        
        paths
    }
    
    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
        let theme: Theme = serde_json::from_str(&content)?;
        theme.validate()?;
        Ok(theme)
    }
    
    pub fn load_by_name(name: &str) -> Result<Self, Error> {
        // Search discovered themes for matching name
        for path in Self::discover_themes() {
            if let Ok(theme) = Self::load(&path) {
                if theme.name == name {
                    return Ok(theme);
                }
            }
        }
        Err(Error::ThemeNotFound(name.to_string()))
    }
}
```

### Validation
```rust
impl Theme {
    fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        // Validate all color strings are valid hex
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.foreground)?;
        self.validate_color(&self.colors.background)?;
        // ... etc
        Ok(())
    }
    
    fn validate_color(s: &str) -> Result<(), Error> {
        if !s.starts_with('#') || s.len() != 7 {
            return Err(Error::InvalidColor(s.to_string()));
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Themes
```rust
impl Theme {
    pub fn dark() -> Self {
        Self {
            name: "dark".into(),
            version: "1.0".into(),
            colors: ThemeColors {
                foreground: "#d4d4d4".into(),
                background: "#1e1e1e".into(),
                accent: "#007acc".into(),
                // ...
            },
            // ...
        }
    }
    
    pub fn light() -> Self {
        // Light theme defaults
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Test: Load valid theme JSON
- Test: Reject invalid JSON
- Test: Reject invalid colors
- Test: Discover themes from directories
- Test: Default themes valid

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Theme struct defined
- [ ] JSON loading works
- [ ] Validation catches bad colors
- [ ] Discovery finds themes
- [ ] Default themes exist","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:32.688625521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:32.695005031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37a","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:39:32.694986045Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-37l","title":"Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP","description":"# Migrate Anthropic provider to asupersync HTTP

## Goal
Replace reqwest usage in src/providers/anthropic.rs with asupersync HTTP client.

## Background
Anthropic is the most complex provider implementation:
- Extended thinking support (streaming thinking deltas)
- Cache control headers (ephemeral caching)
- Tool use with JSON schema
- Multiple content block types in responses
- 748 lines of code, 2 unit tests currently

Current reqwest usage (lines to change):
- Line ~200: reqwest::Client creation
- Line ~250: client.post(url).json(&request)
- Line ~280: response.bytes_stream() for SSE
- Various header handling

## Implementation Steps

1. **Replace Client Creation**
```rust
// Before (reqwest)
let client = reqwest::Client::new();

// After (asupersync)
use crate::http::Client;
let client = Client::new()?;
```

2. **Replace Request Building**
```rust
// Before
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send()
    .await?;

// After
let response = client
    .post(&url)
    .header("x-api-key", &api_key)
    .header("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
    .json(&request)
    .send(&cx)
    .await?;
```

3. **Replace SSE Streaming**
```rust
// Before (reqwest-eventsource or manual)
let mut stream = response.bytes_stream();
while let Some(chunk) = stream.next().await { ... }

// After (asupersync SSE adapter)
use crate::http::SseStream;
let mut sse = SseStream::new(response);
while let Some(event) = sse.next(&cx).await {
    match event? { ... }
}
```

4. **Update Function Signatures**
```rust
// Add Cx parameter to stream()
async fn stream(
    &self,
    cx: &Cx,  // New parameter
    context: &Context,
    options: &StreamOptions,
) -> Result<...>
```

## Anthropic-Specific Considerations
1. **Extended Thinking**: Must handle thinking_start/delta/end events correctly
2. **Cache Headers**: anthropic-beta header for cache control
3. **Error Responses**: Parse error JSON from non-2xx responses
4. **Rate Limiting**: Respect 429 responses with retry-after header
5. **Content Types**: text, thinking, tool_call blocks in single response

## Dependencies
- bd-9sa (HTTP client wrapper)
- bd-pwz (SSE streaming adapter)

## Testing
- Existing 2 unit tests must still pass
- Add integration test with mock SSE server
- Test extended thinking stream parsing
- Test tool call accumulation

## Files
- src/providers/anthropic.rs (modify ~50 lines)
- src/provider.rs (update trait if Cx needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reqwest imports in anthropic.rs
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Streaming works end-to-end
- [ ] Extended thinking parsing works
- [ ] Tool calls parsed correctly
- [ ] Error handling preserved","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:48.745145157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:40.622471805Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-37l","depends_on_id":"bd-gi3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:48.751457930Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-37l","depends_on_id":"bd-pwz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:33:40.622444263Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Goal
Test the interactive TUI's state machine logic - how it responds to messages
and user input events.

## Background
PiApp in src/interactive.rs implements the bubbletea Model trait:
- init() - Initialize state
- update(msg) - Handle messages, return commands
- view() - Render current state

The update() function is the state machine - it needs thorough testing.

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) for each test.
- Log initial state, input message, and key state deltas.
- On failure, dump a compact state diff (selected fields) and the rendered view.

## Test Categories

### 1. Keyboard Input Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_escape_quits() {
    let mut app = create_app();
    let cmd = app.update(KeyMsg::Escape);
    assert!(matches!(cmd, Cmd::Quit));
}
```

- PageUp/PageDown behavior

- bd-1d3 (Snapshot infrastructure for view testing)

- tests/tui_state.rs

- [ ] All PiMsg variants tested

 {"id":"bd-3d8","title":"Implement /theme slash command","description":"# Implement /theme slash command

## Goal
Add /theme command to list available themes and switch between them.

## Command Syntax
```
/theme              - List available themes
/theme dark         - Switch to dark theme
/theme ocean-dark   - Switch to custom theme
```

## Implementation

### Command Handler
```rust
// src/interactive.rs

fn handle_theme_command(&mut self, args: &str) -> Cmd {
    if args.is_empty() {
        // List themes
        let themes = Theme::discover_themes();
        let current = &self.theme.name;
        
        let mut output = String::from("Available themes:\n");
        for path in themes {
            if let Ok(theme) = Theme::load(&path) {
                let marker = if theme.name == *current { "* " } else { "  " };
                output.push_str(&format!("{}{}\n", marker, theme.name));
            }
        }
        output.push_str("\nUse /theme <name> to switch");
        
        self.status_message = Some(output);
        Cmd::None
    } else {
        // Switch theme
        match Theme::load_by_name(args.trim()) {
            Ok(theme) => {
                self.theme = theme.clone();
                self.styles = create_styles(&theme);
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Switched to theme: {}", theme.name));
                
                // Persist preference
                if let Err(e) = self.save_theme_preference(&theme.name) {
                    tracing::warn!("Failed to save theme preference: {}", e);
                }
                
                Cmd::None
            }
            Err(_) => {
                self.status_message = Some(format!("Theme not found: {}", args.trim()));
                Cmd::None
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Persist Theme Preference
```rust
fn save_theme_preference(&self, theme_name: &str) -> Result<()> {
    // Update settings.json with theme preference
    let settings_path = Config::settings_path();
    let mut settings = Config::load_from_file(&settings_path)?;
    settings.theme = Some(theme_name.to_string());
    settings.save_to_file(&settings_path)?;
    Ok(())
}
```

### Add to Slash Command Router
```rust
fn handle_slash_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Cmd {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_slash_command(input);
    
    match cmd {
        "theme" => self.handle_theme_command(args),
        "help" => self.handle_help_command(),
        // ... other commands
    }
}
```

### Update /help Output
```
Commands:
  /help      - Show this help
  /model     - Switch model
  /thinking  - Set thinking level
  /theme     - List/switch themes  <-- NEW
  /clear     - Clear conversation
  /exit      - Exit Pi
```

## Dependencies
- bd-37a (theme loader)
- bd-qpm (theme application)

## Testing
- Test: /theme lists themes
- Test: /theme <name> switches
- Test: Theme persists across restarts
- Test: Invalid theme shows error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] /theme lists available themes
- [ ] /theme <name> switches theme
- [ ] Current theme marked in list
- [ ] Preference saved to settings
- [ ] Invalid theme handled gracefully","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:04.810223066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:11.743557350Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3d8","depends_on_id":"bd-22p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:04.817104772Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3d8","depends_on_id":"bd-qpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:11.743531843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ml","title":"Implement verbose test logging infrastructure","description":"# Implement verbose test logging infrastructure

## Goal
Create shared infrastructure for detailed logging in all integration tests,
enabling easy debugging when tests fail.

## Background
E2E and integration tests need detailed logging to debug failures:
- Every test action should be logged
- Timestamps for timing analysis
- Context (file paths, values) for reproduction
- Logs dumped automatically on test failure

## Implementation

### Log Infrastructure
```rust
// tests/common/logging.rs

use std::sync::Mutex;
use std::time::Instant;

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct LogEntry {
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    pub level: LogLevel,
    pub category: &'static str,
    pub message: String,
    pub context: Vec<(String, String)>,
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum LogLevel {
    Debug,
    Info,
    Warn,
    Error,
}

pub struct TestLogger {
    entries: Mutex<Vec<LogEntry>>,
    start: Instant,
}
```

### Redaction + Artifacts
- Add helpers to redact sensitive values in env/header/context keys (API keys, auth headers).
- Add a simple artifact registry: `record_artifact(name, path)` so tests can attach outputs.
- On panic, dump logs + artifact list (paths) to stderr.

### Test Harness Integration
```rust
// tests/common/harness.rs

pub struct TestHarness {
    pub logger: TestLogger,
    pub temp_dir: TempDir,
}

impl Drop for TestHarness {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        if std::thread::panicking() {
            eprintln!("\n=== TEST LOGS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump());
            eprintln!("=== ARTIFACTS ===\n{}", self.logger.dump_artifacts());
        }
    }
}
```

### Optional File Output
- If `TEST_LOG_PATH` is set, write logs to that path in addition to stderr.

## Files
- tests/common/mod.rs
- tests/common/logging.rs
- tests/common/harness.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TestLogger captures all levels
- [ ] Context key-values supported
- [ ] Timestamps included
- [ ] Redaction helpers present
- [ ] Artifact registry present
- [ ] Auto-dump on test failure
- [ ] Optional file output works
- [ ] All integration tests use harness","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.070756084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124493922Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:33.124430233Z","close_reason":"Implemented redaction helpers, artifact registry + dump, and TEST_LOG_PATH file output in test logging; harness now dumps artifacts on panic and provides record_artifact. Acceptance criteria satisfied.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ml","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:40:54.080417065Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-3ml","author":"QuietBear","text":"Reviewed existing tests/common logger/harness: core logging already present but missing redaction, artifact registry, and TEST_LOG_PATH dump. Implementing those now in tests/common/logging.rs + tests/common/harness.rs (record_artifact, dump_artifacts, redaction helper, env-based file output, artifact dump on panic).","created_at":"2026-02-03T05:48:22Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3nz","title":"Wire session indexing into session.persist()","description":"# Wire session indexing into session.persist()

## Goal
Automatically update the SQLite index when sessions are saved.

## Implementation

### Add Index Update to persist()
```rust
// src/session.rs

impl Session {
    pub async fn persist(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
        // Existing JSONL write logic
        self.write_jsonl(path).await?;
        
        // NEW: Update index
        if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
            if let Err(e) = index.index_session(path) {
                // Log warning but don't fail persist
                tracing::warn!("Failed to update session index: {}", e);
            }
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Default Index Path
```rust
// src/session_index.rs

impl SessionIndex {
    pub fn open_default() -> Result<Self> {
        let path = dirs::data_dir()
            .unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("."))
            .join(".pi/agent/sessions/index.db");
        Self::open(&path)
    }
}
```

### Handle Index Creation
- Create index.db if not exists
- Use WAL mode for concurrent access
- Handle permission errors gracefully

## Considerations
1. **Performance**: Index update should be fast (<10ms)
2. **Reliability**: Don't fail persist if index fails
3. **Concurrency**: Handle multiple pi instances
4. **Migration**: Handle existing sessions without index entries

## Testing
- Test: persist() updates index
- Test: persist() succeeds even if index fails
- Test: Index contains session after persist

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] persist() calls index_session()
- [ ] Index created if not exists
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail persist
- [ ] Performance impact <10ms","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.093344392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:40:58.620167946Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nz","depends_on_id":"bd-346","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:38:32.099125014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3qo","title":"Test infra: mock services + secrets redaction for deterministic runs","description":"Background:
- Extensions often touch network/secrets; tests must be deterministic and safe.

Steps:
- Provide mock HTTP server with canned responses for networked extensions.
- Define secrets injection strategy (env vars + redaction in logs).
- Add helpers to create isolated temp workspaces per scenario.
- Provide deterministic time and random seeds for repeatable runs.

Logging requirements:
- Logs must redact secrets and include mock server request traces.

@@ -55,6 +55,28 @@ jobs:
             exit 1
           fi
 
+      - name: No-mock code guard
+        run: |
+          set -euxo pipefail
+
+          # Allowlisted exceptions (audited):
+          # - MockHttp{Server,Request,Response}: deterministic local TCP server test infra.
+          allow_re='^(MockHttp(Server|Request|Response))$'
+
+          matches="$(rg -n --column --no-heading --color never -o '\b(Mock|Fake|Stub)[A-Za-z0-9_]+\b' tests || true)"
+          if [ -z "$matches" ]; then
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          violations="$(echo "$matches" | awk -F: -v allow_re="$allow_re" '$4 !~ allow_re { print }')"
+          if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
+            echo "$violations"
+            echo
+            echo "No-mock policy violation: Mock*/Fake*/Stub* identifiers are forbidden in tests."
+            echo "Use VCR fixtures or real deps instead. See docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
       - name: cargo fmt
         run: cargo fmt --check
